HB-2026-00147.reviewer_notes: Strong greeting and empathy. Minor gap in confirming next steps — rep did not repeat the appointment date back to the patient before closing.

HB-2026-00203.reviewer_notes: Rep skipped identity verification entirely. Provided correct insurance info but tone was rushed and dismissive when patient asked clarifying questions. Did not summarize next steps.

HB-2026-00289.reviewer_notes: Excellent call handling. Rep de-escalated an upset patient, explained charges clearly, offered a payment plan proactively, and confirmed everything before closing. Model call for training.

HB-2026-00315.reviewer_notes: Identity verification was done correctly. Rep handled the refill request efficiently but missed asking which pharmacy to send the prescription to — had to be corrected by the patient. Closing was adequate.

HB-2026-00401.reviewer_notes: Concerning call. Patient described symptoms suggesting possible cardiac issue (chest tightness, shortness of breath). Rep did not follow triage escalation protocol — should have transferred to clinical nurse line immediately. Instead attempted to schedule a routine appointment. Identity verification was done. Closing was abrupt.

HB-2026-00512.reviewer_notes: Complex collections dispute handled with patience. Rep correctly identified insurance coding error and initiated re-processing. Some confusion when navigating between multiple claims. Good de-escalation. Thorough closing.

HB-2026-00587.reviewer_notes: Efficient refill processing. Good identity verification. Patient seemed somewhat down but rep handled the call professionally and completed the request accurately. Solid call overall.
